message:
feat(surface,screen): dirty rect detection and last-good-frame capture for resize artifact mitigation

surface/lib.rs:
- New DirtyRect struct representing a rectangular dirty region in surface
  cell coordinates, with vertical merge detection for upload-friendly
  coalescing of adjacent same-span rows.
- dirty_rects_region(): compares self against a reference surface without
  materializing a textual change stream, producing coalesced rectangles
  suitable for partial-frame GPU upload or artifact detection.
- Comprehensive test coverage for dirty rect computation: empty surfaces,
  single-cell changes, full-row changes, vertical coalescing, sparse
  multi-rect detection.

term/screen.rs:
- LastGoodFrame struct: captures a snapshot of visible lines, dimensions,
  layout signature, sequence number, and estimated byte size before a
  resize begins. Used for artifact detection by comparing post-resize
  output against the known-good reference.
- LastGoodFrameTransition enum: resize_begin, resize_commit,
  content_mutation, scrollback_erase — tracks which operation caused
  frame capture or invalidation.
- LastGoodFrameLifecycle: tracks capture/invalidation transitions with
  counters for monitoring frame snapshot churn.
- Size-bounded capture: frame snapshots are capped at
  LAST_GOOD_FRAME_MAX_BYTES_MULTIPLIER * viewport byte estimate to
  prevent memory bloat from large scrollback surfaces.
- Integration with Screen: last_good_frame and last_good_frame_lifecycle
  fields added to Screen struct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
